manuscript_profile.title: Ancient Bloodlines
manuscript_profile.target_audience: Middle-grade readers (ages 8–12) interested in animal fantasy, environmental themes, and anti-bullying narratives

required_public_strings: Cross-species relationship engine built through action rather than exposition.
Ecological authenticity grounding the fantasy in observable natural systems.
Tonal instability between gentle middle-grade fable and graphic violence.
Author retains ownership of manuscript content
optional_public_strings: Thorander's creation myth monologue (~2,000 words of uninterrupted backstory).
Narrative momentum halts completely during a static lecture scene.
Restructure as interrupted storytelling with counterpoint tension.
Maintains engagement through the middle third while still delivering essential world-building.
Do not cut the creation myth entirely; it establishes the thematic foundation for cross-species cooperation.
source_only_strings: Newton, a young rough-skinned newt with a damaged leg, must survive bullying, predation, and species prejudice to forge an unprecedented cross-species alliance.
